notable_pick_trades: Sent 2026 5th-round pick to Ravens in October 2025 trade for Odafe Oweh. Sent 2026 7th-round pick to Titans in August 2024 trade for Elijah Molden. No compensatory picks awarded.
roster_context: The Chargers went 11-6 in 2025 and lost in the Wild Card round to the Patriots. The offense was ravaged by OL injuries (lost Rashawn Slater and Joe Alt for extended stretches), leading to one of the worst offensive lines in the NFL. Both tackles return healthy for 2026, but the entire interior OL has been overhauled after Bradley Bozeman retired, Zion Johnson left in free agency, and Mekhi Becton was released. New OC Mike McDaniel replaces Greg Roman, and new DC Chris O'Leary replaces Jesse Minter (now Ravens HC). The defense remains strong but lost key edge rusher Odafe Oweh to Washington's $100M deal. Only 5 draft picks limits flexibility.

needs.explanation: The entire interior OL has been rebuilt. Bradley Bozeman retired. Zion Johnson signed with the Browns ($49.5M). Mekhi Becton was released. The Chargers signed Tyler Biadasz at center and Cole Strange as a guard, but Strange is a floor raiser on a modest $13M deal, not a proven starter. The second guard spot is completely open with Trevor Penning as the only fallback option. The Chargers fielded the lowest-graded interior in the NFL in 2025 and must add a Day 1 starting guard to protect Justin Herbert.
needs.current_starter: Cole Strange (LG), Tyler Biadasz (C), TBD (RG — Penning or draft pick)
needs.depth: Trevor Penning has guard experience but profiles as swing tackle. No other viable interior options on roster.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2. Multiple analysts project IOL at No. 22 overall (Vega Ioane, Kadyn Proctor, Spencer Fano). If not Round 1, must address in Round 2.

needs.explanation: Odafe Oweh signed a 4-year, $100M deal with Washington after a dominant half-season with the Chargers (7.5 sacks in 12 regular season games). Khalil Mack was re-signed on a 1-year, $18M deal but turns 35 and this is likely his final season. Tuli Tuipulotu is in the final year of his rookie deal and is the long-term piece. The team needs a third edge rusher to replace Oweh's production and plan for Mack's retirement.
needs.depth: Kyle Kennard, Caleb Murphy — rotational pieces only. No proven third option.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2. Deep edge class makes this a strong value play if IOL is addressed first.

needs.explanation: The Chargers re-signed Teair Tart on a multi-year extension and added veteran Dalvin Tomlinson on a 1-year, $7.5M deal. Jamaree Caldwell (2025 3rd-round pick) flashed as a rookie. But Da'Shawn Hand left for Atlanta and Otito Ogbonnia signed with Dallas. The interior DL lacks a true disruptive 3-technique pass rusher. Tomlinson is 32 and graded 120th of 134 DTs at PFF last season. The defense needs more interior beef, especially when Tart is off the field.
needs.depth: Jamaree Caldwell showed promise. Justin Eboigbe is a depth piece.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-3. Multiple mock drafts project DT to Chargers at 22 (Peter Woods, Caleb Banks, Kayden McDonald).

needs.explanation: Cam Hart and Tarheeb Still are promising young starters from the 2024 draft class, but the Chargers lost key depth CB Benjamin St-Juste (signed with Packers) and Ja'Sir Taylor is a free agent. Donte Jackson is under contract but aging. Hart dealt with durability concerns (concussion, ankle sprains, torn labrum). The cornerback room has potential but lacks proven depth behind the starters, especially for a defense that uses a lot of sub-packages.
needs.current_starter: Cam Hart, Tarheeb Still, Donte Jackson (slot)
needs.depth: Deane Leonard (re-signed, primarily ST), Nikko Reed. Thin behind starters.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-3. New DC Chris O'Leary wants press-man corners. Analysts have mocked CB Avieon Terrell and Brandon Cisse to LAC.

needs.explanation: Ladd McConkey is the WR1 but no Charger cracked 800 receiving yards in 2025. Quentin Johnston has been discussed in trade talks and produced only 735 yards as the nominal WR1. Keenan Allen is a UFA at age 34 and unsigned. Tre Harris showed some promise as a rookie but tore his Achilles in Week 3. The team lacks a true outside X-receiver. Even if Allen returns on a cheap deal, the Chargers need a dynamic weapon to pair with McConkey in Mike McDaniel's new scheme.
needs.depth: Tre Harris (returning from Achilles), KeAndre Lambert-Smith, Derius Davis. Keenan Allen unsigned but expected to return on cheap deal.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-3. WR coach was spotted heavily scouting at the Combine. Could target a true X-receiver.

needs.explanation: Derwin James Jr. is an All-Pro but enters the final year of his contract and plays more of a nickel/slot corner role in this defense. Elijah Molden is the other starting safety. Alohi Gilman was traded to Baltimore in the Oweh deal. Tony Jefferson is an unsigned free agent at 33. The safety room needs a young developmental piece even if the starters are adequate, especially to plan for life after James if an extension doesn't happen.
needs.depth: Kendall Williamson (extended), R.J. Mickens. Tony Jefferson unsigned.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 3-5. Deep safety class in 2026 offers value later in the draft.

needs.explanation: Daiyan Henley is the starter and showed growth in Year 2. Denzel Perryman was re-signed but is 33 years old and more of a locker room leader at this stage. Del'Shawn Phillips was re-signed as a special teams ace. The Chargers lack a young, athletic three-down LB who can cover in today's NFL. This is a historically deep LB draft class.
needs.depth: Del'Shawn Phillips (special teams), Marlowe Wax, Troy Dye
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-4. Elite 2026 LB class could allow Chargers to find value even later.

free_agency_impact: Lost Odafe Oweh (EDGE) to Commanders — 4yr/$100M. Creates critical EDGE need and need for 3rd edge rusher.
Lost Zion Johnson (LG) to Browns — 3yr/$49.5M. Leaves massive hole at guard.
Lost Benjamin St-Juste (CB) to Packers — reduces CB depth.
Lost Tyler Conklin (TE) to Lions — minor, was not a primary contributor.
Lost Da'Shawn Hand (DT) to Falcons — 1yr/$3M. Reduces DL depth.
Lost Otito Ogbonnia (DT) to Cowboys — reduces DL depth further.
Lost Jamaree Salyer (OT) to Dolphins — was emergency OL option.
Bradley Bozeman retired (C) — necessitated Biadasz signing.
Released Mekhi Becton (RG) — saves ~$10M cap, opens RG spot.
Released Will Dissly (TE) — created TE need filled by Kolar.
Re-signed Khalil Mack — 1yr/$18M fully guaranteed. Stabilizes EDGE for 2026 but not long-term.
Signed Tyler Biadasz (C) — 3yr/$30M. Fills center void left by Bozeman's retirement.
Signed Cole Strange (G) — 2yr/$13M. Possible starting guard but not proven high-level starter.
Signed Charlie Kolar (TE) — 3yr/$24.3M. Run-blocking TE to pair with Gadsden.
Signed Dalvin Tomlinson (DT) — 1yr/$7.5M. Veteran depth behind Tart.
Signed Alec Ingold (FB) — reunites with OC Mike McDaniel from Miami.
Re-signed Trey Pipkins (OT) — 2yr/$10M. Swing tackle depth.
Re-signed Trevor Penning (OT/G) — 1yr deal. Guard/tackle versatility.
Re-signed Denzel Perryman (LB), Del'Shawn Phillips (LB/ST), Deane Leonard (CB/ST), Josh Harris (LS).
Re-signed Trey Lance (QB) — 1yr backup deal behind Herbert.
Signed Keaton Mitchell (RB) — 2yr deal. Speed complement to Hampton/Vidal.
Tendered Kimani Vidal (RB) as ERFA.
Extended Teair Tart (DT) pre-FA — multi-year, up to $37.5M. Anchors DL.
Keenan Allen (WR) remains unsigned UFA — expected to return on cheap deal but not yet done.
Quentin Johnston (WR) reportedly a trade discussion topic per ESPN's Fowler.
scheme_fit_notes: New OC Mike McDaniel brings a wide-zone, motion-heavy scheme from Miami that prioritizes athletic, mobile OL who can pull and get to the second level — making IOL athleticism paramount. New DC Chris O'Leary is a Jesse Minter protege continuing the same aggressive, multiple-front defense that led the NFL in points allowed in 2024. The defense uses heavy sub-packages with Derwin James in a hybrid nickel role, meaning versatile CBs and coverage-capable LBs are valued. Press-man corners fit the scheme. Edge rushers who can set the edge against the run and rush the passer are premium. Interior DL who can eat double-teams and generate interior pressure are needed to complement the coverage scheme.
